name: GM Turbo Hydramatic 400 (TH400)
key_features: Heavy-duty 3-speed used behind large big-block engines. Bulletproof strength, popular in high-horsepower drag builds.

name: GM Turbo Hydramatic 350 (TH350)
key_features: Lighter and more compact than the TH400. Extremely common behind small-block V8s; highly versatile and robs less power than the TH400.

name: Chrysler TorqueFlite 727
key_features: Mopar's equivalent to the TH400. Sat behind the 426 Hemi and 440 Six Pack and is considered one of the strongest factory automatics.

name: Ford C6
key_features: Ford's heavy-duty 3-speed automatic used behind massive FE, 385-series big blocks, and the 351 Cleveland.

name: GM Powerglide
key_features: Two-speed automatic originally introduced in the 1950s. Extreme simplicity and strength make it a staple in dedicated drag racing.

name: Muncie M20 (Wide Ratio)
key_features: The standard wide-ratio manual for GM muscle cars, designed for street cars with taller rear-end gears.

name: Muncie M21 / M22 (Close Ratio)
key_features: The heavy-duty M22 ('Rock Crusher') is known for the whining noise of its straight-cut gears.

name: Ford Toploader (Wide Ratio)
key_features: Named because the internal gears were loaded through the top of the case. Incredibly strong and used in cars like Mustangs and Fairlanes.

name: Ford Toploader (Close Ratio)
key_features: Close-ratio variant of Ford's indestructible 4-speed, utilized behind the highest output Total Performance V8s.

name: Chrysler A833 4-Speed
key_features: The go-to heavy-duty manual transmission for Plymouth and Dodge muscle cars, known for being virtually indestructible in factory form.

name: GM 4L60E
key_features: Modern, electronically controlled 4-speed that evolved from the 700R4. Features a deep first gear for acceleration.

name: GM 4L80E
key_features: Essentially an electronically controlled TH400 with an overdrive gear. Heavily favored for high-horsepower LS swaps.

name: GM 6L80E
key_features: A six-speed automatic that offers a steep first gear for acceleration and double overdrives for cruising.

name: ZF 8HP Series (e.g., 8HP75)
key_features: A modern 8-speed automatic adapted into classic cars for its lightning-fast shifts and excellent close-ratio gear spacing.

name: Tremec T-56 Magnum
key_features: The most popular modern aftermarket manual swap. A six-speed capable of handling extreme torque, giving close-ratio acceleration and two overdrive gears.

name: Tremec TKX
key_features: A five-speed manual specifically designed to fit inside the narrow transmission tunnels of classic muscle cars without requiring floorboard modifications.

name: Tremec TR6060
key_features: The heavy-duty factory OEM successor to the T-56. Features wider gears and improved synchronizers. Found natively in modern muscle like the Camaro SS, Challenger, and Shelby GT500.
